text.title: Rust Tokio Async Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Rust Tokio Async methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Cargo features MUST declare runtime flavour (`tokio = { version='1', features=['rt-multi-thread', ...] }`) and runtime build MUST configure worker count explicitly; default-runtime builds in production are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Default `multi_thread` chooses worker_count = CPU cores, which may be wrong for the workload; explicit choice surfaces the decision.

rule.statement: Async functions MUST NOT call `std::fs`, `std::thread::sleep`, blocking sync mutexes, or CPU loops >1ms; blocking work MUST run inside `tokio::task::spawn_blocking`.
rule.rationale: Blocking calls stall the worker thread, freezing all tasks on it; spawn_blocking moves them to a dedicated blocking pool.

rule.statement: Every network `await` MUST be wrapped in `tokio::time::timeout(...)`; bare `await` on a wire I/O future is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without timeout, a hung upstream blocks the task forever; timeout returns `Err(Elapsed)` and frees the slot.

rule.statement: `mpsc::channel(capacity)` MUST use a bounded capacity sized to load; `mpsc::unbounded_channel` is forbidden in production paths.
rule.rationale: Unbounded channels accumulate if consumer is slower than producer; bounded back-pressures the producer.

rule.statement: Multi-task fan-out MUST use `JoinSet` or `tokio::select!` with cancel-safe arms; raw `tokio::spawn` with dropped JoinHandle is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Dropped handles leak tasks silently; JoinSet propagates panics + bounds lifetime; select! arms must be cancel-safe or selection drops in-flight work.

rule.statement: `std::sync::Mutex` MUST NOT be held across `.await`; use `tokio::sync::Mutex` for guarded async sections or restructure so the lock drops before await.
rule.rationale: Holding a sync mutex across await blocks the worker; holding the lock through an async section can deadlock the runtime.

rule.statement: Service MUST listen for `ctrl_c` (or signal stream) + propagate cancellation via `CancellationToken` or shutdown channel; abrupt drop without cleanup is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without coordinated shutdown, in-flight requests are dropped mid-write; clients see TCP resets and partial data.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec - decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
